texts.full_text: (RBC) theory, which focused on the role of technology shocks and other supply-side factors in causing business cycles. But these models also had their limitations. They struggled to explain the financial crises of the early 2000s, such as the dot-com bubble and the subprime mortgage crisis. They also tended to downplay the importance of demand-side factors, such as changes in consumer confidence and investment spending, in driving economic fluctuations. In light of these shortcomings, it's clear that macroeconomics needs a new research program that can better capture the complexities of real-world economies. This program should incorporate insights from other fields, such as psychology, sociology, and political science, to develop more realistic models of human behaviour and institutional arrangements. Using industry standard monitoring and remote access tools, BSS provides 24x7x365 professional monitoring of your servers, PCs, switches and firewalls. On a daily basis, a BSS technician will check the completion of your backup job and complete periodic backup checks. Backup/Disaster Recovery The worst time to discover that your backup and disaster recovery plan is inadequate is after the disaster has occurred. Companies may think they have a strong backup with redundancy only to find out it hasn't been operational for months. Others find out the hard way that staff have not been properly saving data to the server to be backed up daily. When their PC or notebook crashes, suddenly panic sets in. There are several key questions to consider when developing your backup and disaster recovery strategies. “Should we maintain our premise based backup or go totally to the cloud?” “How much data can we afford to lose in a disaster?” “How quickly do we need to be back up and fully operational?” Boston Systems will walk you through the “pros and cons” of cloud vs. premise based backup and disaster recovery solutions. texts.full_text: e following locking mechanism: # Convert FLAC to MP3 using tags from flac file. # if [ ! -e $FLAC.lock ]; then touch $FLAC.lock flac -dc "$FLAC" | lame${lame_opts} \ --tt "$TITLE" \ --tn "$TRACKNUMBER" \ --tg "$GENRE" \ --ty "$DATE" \ --ta "$ARTIST" \ --tl "$ALBUM" \ --add-id3v2 \ - "$MP3" rm $FLAC.lock fi; However, this still leaves a race condition. A: The "lOckfile" command provides what you're trying to do for shell scripts without the race condition. The command was written by the procmail folks specifically for this sort of purpose and is available on most BSD/Linux systems (as procmail is available for most environments). Your test becomes something like this: lockfile -r 3 $FLAC.lock if test $? -eq 0 ; then flac -dc "$FLAC" | lame${lame_opts} \ --tt "$TITLE" \ --tn "$TRACKNUMBER" \ --tg "$GENRE" \ --ty "$DATE" \ --ta "$ARTIST" \ --tl "$ALBUM" \ --add-id3v2 \ - "$MP3" fi rm -f $FLAC.lock Alternatively, you could make lockfile keep retrying indefinitely so you don't need to test the return code, and instead can test for the output file for determining whether to run flac. A: If you don't have lockfile and cannot install it (in any of its versions - there are several implementations) a robust and portable atomic mutex is mkdir. An informal communique concerningdevelopments, events and announcements in theRoyal Heraldry Society of Canada Friday, May 11, 2012 Queen's Diamond Jubilee Medal Awarded to Members of the Society NANAIMO, BC, May 11, 2012 - The Society, the President, the Chair of the Honours & Awards Committee (Robert D. Watt) and the committee members wish to convey congratulations to the members awarded the Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal. The RHSC was allotted an incredible 37 medals. These were carefully distributed as a result of numerous discussions by the H&AC members, Robb Watt and the Society's President for weeks during the earlier part of this year. The selection process employed was one that was fair and efficient, allowing the Society to acknowledge worthy individuals and to submit the citations in a timely manner. A special thank you must be conveyed to the Usher of the Black Rod and Canadian Secretary to the Queen, Kevin MacLeod who was our special guest at the banquet, Saturday evening on May 5 at the RHSC 46th AGM and Conference in Nanaimo. He not only delivered an engaging speech, he also posed for a series of portraits of his presenting the Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal to each of the recipients present at the dinner folled by photo opportunites with Kevin MacLeod who was so gracious as our presenter that we hope one day there will be time and place where it is possible meet him again. texts.full_text: a continuous fastening surface, which is obtained on three sides of the floor. Furthermore by providing multiple attachment points around and within each side's interior area. In modern automotive design, weight reduction while maintaining structural integrity remains an important goal to improve fuel efficiency without compromising safety standards or performance characteristics like handling stability and crashworthiness. The use of lightweight materials such as aluminum alloys has become increasingly popular in vehicle body constructions due to their favorable strength-to-weight ratio compared with steel. However, the transition from heavier steels to lighter metals introduces new challenges that must be addressed by designers when it comes to structural rigidity and durability—particularly concerning floor structures. These lightweight materials can exhibit mechanical properties than those of conventional mild or high-strength steels used in vehicle constructions for decades; thus requiring a reevaluation of traditional design approaches. To mitigate the potential drawbacks associated with these new material choices, it is critical to optimize both component geometry and assembly techniques—such as adhesive bonding strategies. This can be achieved by leveraging computational models that simulate real-world loading conditions on vehicle components under various operating scenarios (e.g., acceleration/deceleration forces during braking or cornering maneuvers) while accounting for material-specific behaviors. By employing such advanced modeling approaches, engineers are able to identify areas of concern where additional reinforcement may be necessary without the need for physical prototypes. texts.full_text: Push Anti-Immigrant Rhetoric On Sunday morning, Indianapolis Colts linebacker Edwin Jackson died in a fatal car accident after being struck by a suspected drunk driver. Donald Trump, the bigoted opportunist that he is, decided to use the 26-year-old’s death for his immigration platform. According to Huffpost, authorities discovered that the driver responsible for Jackson’s death is Manuel Orrego-Savala, a Guatemalan citizen who is in the U.S. illegally. Trump took to Twitter to express his condolences to Jackson's family but quickly pivoted to his anti-immigration stance, highlighting the driver's immigration status. "So disgraceful that a person illegally in our country killed @Colts linebacker Edwin Jackson," Trump tweeted. "This is just one of many such preventable tragedies. We must get the Dems to get tough on the Border, and with illegal immigration, FAST!" Trump's tweet sparked immediate backlash, with many criticizing him for politicizing a tragedy to push his agenda. Some pointed out that focusing on the driver's immigration status distracts from the real issue of drunk driving, while others highlighted the President's silence when similar incidents involve American citizens. The Colts organization released a statement expressing their condolences and support for Jackson's family, emphasizing his contributions to the team and the community. "We were heartbroken to hear the news of Edwin Jackson’s passing," the statement read. "Edwin was loved by all in the Colts organization. We admired his outgoing personality, competitive spirit and hard-working mentality.

texts.full_text: can reside on different IP subnets, function as IP routers to the mobile units and communicate with the AR using IP packets instead of link-layer frames. One of the main design issues in the deployment of 802.11 WLANs is the issue of seamless roaming support for mobile units within the wireless network. Mobility has to be supported without any, or at least a minimum of, service disruptions. For example, application level sessions should not be disrupted during handoffs between APs that provide link-layer connectivity to the mobile unit. If the APs are within the same IP subnet, a layer-2 handoff mechanism, inherent in 802.11 WLANs, would be sufficient to handle seamless mobility. If the APs are attached to different IP subnets, however, layer-3 handoff mechanisms would need to be employed to support the seamless mobility. Layer-2 mechanisms have limitations. For instance, deployment in a wide area, linking every AP to one cable or one network presents an issue. It is more desirable to place the APs in the IP (or network)

The LGBT community in Atlanta has significant political influence, and the firm quickly became a target for major gay rights organizations including the Human Rights Campaign and the group Georgia Equality -- the largest gay rights advocate in the state. The groups planned an aggressive ad campaign, direct communication with the firm's clients, and a diminution of its Corporate Equality Index ranking -- the metric HRC uses to track corporate support for gay righst. The decision was also met by internal dissension at King & Spalding -- one partner told TPM that many associates were upset about being "dragged into" what they considered an unpopular case. No new government has been formed following December elections. http://yhoo.it/1Oj2bYc EUROCRAT JET-SETTERS: Commission staff showed restraint in 2015, taking just 97,318 work trips. That’s down from 101,919 in 2014, according to written answers from the Commission to MEP questions. Given that most “assistants” (AST grade staff in Commission-speak) never travel for work, that leaves 13,590 staff at the higher “adviser” (AD) grade taking the bulk of the 97,318 trips. If we grant that every assistant may take one trip per Human: year on average and that all 13,590 ADs travel for work, then we can calculate the number of trips taken by each AD in 2015 to be around 7.15. That’s quite a bit more than the average assistant takes, which is just over one trip per year. It's worth noting that these figures don't take into account the cost of these trips, which can be significant for an organization as large and complex as the European Commission. However, it's also worth noting that the number of trips taken by EU officials has been on a downward trend in recent years, which is a positive sign. Overall, while the number of trips taken by EU officials may seem high, it's important to remember that these officials are working across multiple countries and often need to travel for meetings, negotiations,

texts.full_text: atin therapy was initiated according to guidelines for cardiovascular protection. Serum creatinine concentration and estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) were monitored as primary indicators of kidney health post-transplantation. Studies suggest that statins may exert beneficial effects on renal allograft function beyond their lipid-lowering properties, possibly through improving endothelial cell integrity or reducing inflammation. These pleiotropic actions could contribute to enhanced graft survival and better overall outcomes for transplant recipients by mitigating the risk factors associated with chronic kidney disease progression in this population. While data is promising regarding statin use post-transplantation due primarily to their cardiovascular benefits—such as reduced incidence of myocardial infarction, stroke, or death from cardiac causes—their specific impact on renal allograft function warrants further investigation. Future studies should aim at clarifying the mechanisms by which these agents may confer additional protective effects in this context. Additionally, monitoring for potential adverse events related to statin therapy remains essential since patients with transplanted kidneys often have a complex medication regimen and are susceptible to drug interactions that could affect renal outcomes or lead to side-effects like myopathy. Clinicians must balance the benefits of cardiovascular protection against any possible risks when prescribing these medications. texts.full_text: pragmatic abilities of children: Part 2. Guidelines, considerations, and specific evaluation procedures. General guidelines are presented for the assessment ofpragmatic communication skills in young children (ages birth to six years) with various developmental disabilities or delays. These guidelines aim to support professionals working on early intervention programs by providing a systematic approach when evaluating and interpreting pragmatic abilities, which involve understanding social rules for verbal and nonverbal interactions within everyday contexts such as family routines, play activities, childcare environments, and community settings (Browder et al., 2013). When assessing pragmatics in young children with developmental delays or disabilities, it is essential to consider several factors that may influence communication skills development: cultural background, the presence of other medical conditions affecting speech-language abilities, cognitive functioning levels, sensory processing challenges (e.g., hearing loss and visual impairment), emotional regulation issues, as well social experiences within home life and early childhood educatio

texts.full_text: e events after the rodeo each night: the show and the dance. When the actual rodeo competition ends each night, the gates of the arena are opened and thousands of people rush in to watch the show. By show I mean concert, each night by a different and very famous country singer. If anyone has ever heard the song Ai Se Eu Te Pego, which was an international hit and one I was singing before I even got here.. Yep! That singer came and sang in Palmital. I even got within a few yards of the stage that night! The shows start around 11-midnight and go on for a couple of hours. The best part is that all of this..the rodeo and the show, is completely free and put on by the city! The only part of the rodeo I had to pay for was the dance which happens each night after the show. So yes, the party starts around 2-3 AM and lasts until 6-7. I´ll never forget walking into my house at 7:30 AM when it was totally light out already and thinking "Wow, normally I would be starting school right now". Brazilians, man. Anyways I can feel how quickly my year is winding down which can only be described as bittersweet. I think leaving Brazil will be one of the hardest things I ever do. I have gotten so attached to my life here and the people in it. On the other hand, I have begun to feel like I'm just sort of spinning my wheels here, and I know that I have new and exciting things ahead of me back in the US. It doesn't hurt either

texts.full_text: Genial Bone Advancement Trephine system in conjunction with uvulopalatopharyngoplasty in the multilevel management of obstructive sleep apnea. The successful surgical management of obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) requires surgical procedures that address both retropalatal and retrolingual airway obstruction. The Genial Bone Advancement Trephine (GBAT) system is a new, 1-step system that allows for isolation and advancement of the genioglossus muscle via a guided trephine system. The purpose of this project was to describe our experience using the GBAT system to perform genioglossus advancement (GGA) in conjunction with uvulopalatopharyngoplasty (UPPP) in the surgical management of OSA. Retrospective analysis of 35 consecutive patients undergoing GGA using the GBAT system in conjunction with UPPP for the management of OSA during a 3-year period (1999 to 2002). Twenty-four patients had complete preoperative and postoperative polysomnographic data. A 70% reduction in the Respiratory Disturbance Index (RDI) and Apnea Index (AI) in the preoperative versus postoperative PSG (RDI, 52.9 +/- 17.1 versus 15.9 +/- 7.5; AI, 20.1 +/- 6.7 versus 6.1 +/- 4.5; P < or = 0.0001). The lowest oxygen desaturation increased from 80% to 88% (P = 0.03), and the mean saturation of hemoglobin with oxygen improved significantly, rising from 92.6% ± 1.7% preoperatively to 94.5% ± 1.2% postoperatively (P < or = 0.01). Subjective symptoms also showed significant improvement: snoring decreased in intensity and frequency as reported by bed partners, Epworth Sleepiness Scale scores dropped from an average of 13.8 preoperatively to 7.5 postoperatively (P < or = 0.001), indicating a substantial reduction in daytime sleepiness. Complications were minimal; one patient experienced temporary hypoesthesia around the chin area due to manipulation during surgery, which resolved within six months without further intervention. No patients reported significant changes in speech articulation or swallowing difficulties postoperatively. In conclusion, combining genioglossus advancement using the GBAT system with uvulopalatopharyngoplasty is an effective and safe surgical approach for treating multilevel obstructive sleep apnea. The procedure offers a substantial improvement in both objective measures of airway obstruction as well as subjective quality-of-life outcomes, supporting its consideration in patients who are candidates for surgery to manage

The Dow Jones industrial average is down 2 percent, putting it on course for one of its worst one-day drops following a U.S. presidential election. The biggest, in 2008, came in the midst of the financial crisis on the day after President Barack Obama won his first term. The men whose victories did really bad things to the stock market like Obama’s? FDR and his corrupt Vice President, Harry Truman. And before you start thinking cliches about the Greatest Generation, FDR didn’t save America from a depression, he buried it deep inside one, until a global war shook it out. And there’s more good news for market-watchers who were looking for a similar fate: The so-called "economic cliff" looms forward, and without a deal in Congress, automatic budget cuts and revenue increases will kick in at the start of the new decade. This will send the economy reeling and plunge the stock market further into depression. Investors are often warned about the Eurozone debt situation, which has been far from addressed, and how it could impact the U.S. economy. With Trump's re-election, there's a feeling of continuity in policy, but if that's sufficient to steer the ship through stormy seas remains to be seen. As ever, it's important to know that stock market changes are normal, and while this drop is huge, it's not unprecedented. Savvy analysts believe that staying the course and taking rational choices based on long-term objectives is always the best approach during periods of uncertainty. Only time will decide how the market will behave in the coming years and weeks, but one fact is obvious: the election results have spoken, and the political future of the nation remains a hot topic for discussion and speculation. texts.full_text: in the British Army and now a charity director and Governor of the Military Knights of Windsor. Early life Hobbs was born 28 February 1937. He was educated at Eton College. Military career Hobbs was commissioned into the Grenadier Guards on 14 April 1956 as a second lieutenant to fulfil his national service obligation. He was promoted to lieutenant on 28 February 1958 and was removed from the national service list. On 28 February 1964, he was promoted to captain, and to major on 31 December 1969. He served on the Directing Staff of Staff College, Camberley between 1974 and 1977. In 1979, during The Troubles, he was posted to Northern Ireland. On 30 June 1980, Hobbs was promoted to colonel, and to brigadier on 31 December 1981. He was appointed Commander of the 39th Infantry Brigade in 1982. He was posted again to Northern Ireland in 1983 as part of the Staff. He was appointed Director of Army Public Relations at the Ministry of Defence and served there from January until December, during which he played a key role managing media relations for military operations across various theatres including conflicts like The Troubles or other significant events impacting Britain's armed forces reputation both domestically & internationally. Hobbs was promoted to major general on New Year’ Eve of '83-'84 overseeing the formation and training aspects within his rank before eventually retiring from active service in 1992 after a nearly four-decade long illustrious career marked by dedication, courage under fire during numerous deployments across various operational theatres including command positions at home & abroad. In addition to these responsibilities post retirement Michael Hobbs also took up roles as charity director and governor of the Military Knights Of Windsor where he has continued contributing towards community development initiatives primarily focusing on welfare programs for serving members past or present within British Army helping them navigate through personal challenges they might face during their transition into civilian life offering support

texts.full_text: lt: Edit: Typically you won't want to plug in the normal map node. I did that because there were some shading issues that cropped up in Eevee, and it seemed to fix it. In cycles, it works fine without the Normal Map node. A: Apologies to @stphnl329 for a very similar answer, but I got this far, and maybe there are a couple of extra points here... If the pattern on the reference image encodes anything, it encodes height, not normals. Agreed with @stphnl329, it's good to make a clean extraction of the green pixels as a black and white mask. (@stphnl's is better than mine) Remember that your references are used for dimensions like UDIM or PBR textures where you don't have control over how many UVs per face. You can paint something more complicated on top. The effect works pretty well if it sticks to a single color (Black in this case), and maybe with minor adjustments, the results will be better. It's probably enough for most use cases that only need resolution high compared to low textures as one or two brushes full of strokes like an open mesh generator but also stuff similar. You have more flexibility at doing something if you do not see individual pixels so remember how complex it is in a final render. And this image has very hard edges. This makes some patterns possible that would be impossible with soft, fuzzy shapes...

texts.full_text: ce policymakers to open the public purse. In it, the multiplier analysis is the most commonly called play. Residents expect demonstrable economic results from policymakers — especially with a 24-hour news cycle to account for their policies’ productivity. Thus, every state legislator is accountable to monthly employment statistics. Monthly employment fluctuations, however, are rarely caused solely by policymakers' actions, but rather by the complex interplay of economic forces. Regardless, Lansing insiders hold policymakers to a higher standard: proof is required that a politician’s policies pushed the economic needle. This environment creates a market for abusing the multiplier analysis. These analyses are manipulative because every economic action creates secondary and tertiary consequences.
